# @meli/i18n

Manage i18n (internationalization) in your NodeJS app.

## Description

This module provides the `I18n` class to translate your app's wordings according to the translations and language provided, as well as an ExpressJS middleware to easily setup the translations.

## Requirements

The I18n CLI (`@meli-dev/i18n-tools`) **development** dependency is required to gather and upload your app's wordings, and then download their translations. Please refer to [its docs](https://github.com/melisource/fury_node-i18n-dev-tools) for more information.

## Install

```shell
npm install --save @meli/i18n
```

## Usage

### `I18n` Class

Use the `I18n` class to get the translation for a certain wording, according the the `translations` object provided on init.

#### `I18n` Initialization

```js
import { I18n } from '@meli/i18n';

const i18n = new I18n({ translations });
```

##### `I18n` Initialization Options

| Property                    | Required |               Type               | Description                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| --------------------------- | :------: | :------------------------------: | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `translations`              |    ✅    | [`Translations`](./src/types.ts) | The translation map that will be used to get the translation for the current instance. This object is usually read from the files downloaded with the `@meli-dev/i18n-tools` for each locale. |
| `locale`                    |          |    [`Locale`](./src/types.ts)    | The current locale to which the translations corresponde to. Used to send metrics about missing translations if detected.                                                                     |
| `notifyMissingTranslations` |          |            `boolean`             | Choose if you wish to send metrics about missing translations in your project for the current locale. Defaults to `false`.                                                                    |

#### `i18n.gettext()`

Given a text, return the translation with its arguments replaced.

Params:

- `text: string` - The text to translate.
- `replacements: Array<string | number>` - Array with the replacements for `{n}` variables.

```js
i18n.gettext('These are {0} and {1}, right {0}?', ['John', 'Jessica']);
// => These are John and Jessica, right John?
```

#### `i18n.ngettext()`

Given a text in singular, a text in plural and a quantity, return the translation with its arguments replaced.

Params:

- `singularText: string` - The text in singular form to translate.
- `pluralText: string` - The text un plural form to translate.
- `quantity: number` - Determines which state to use (singular/plural). Uses singular with value `1` and plural with `>1`.
- `replacements: Array<string | number>` - Array with the replacements for `{n}` variables.

```js
i18n.ngettext('{0}, You have {1} message', '{0}, You have {1} messages', 6, [
  'John',
  6,
]);
// => John, You have 6 messages
```

#### `i18n.pgettext()`

Given a context and a text, return the translation with its arguments replaced.

Params:

- `context: string` - The context representing this text key.
- `text: string` - The text to translate.
- `replacements: Array<string | number>` - Array with the replacements for `{n}` variables.

```js
i18n.pgettext('context-to-use', 'This is {0}, right {0}?', ['John']);
// => This is John, right John?
```

#### `i18n.npgettext()`

Given a context, a text in singular, a text in plural and a quantity, return the translation with its arguments replaced.

Params:

- `context: string` - The context representing this text key.
- `singularText: string` - The text in singular form to translate.
- `pluralText: string` - The text un plural form to translate.
- `quantity: number` - Determines which state to use (singular/plural). Uses singular with value `1` and plural with `>1`.
- `replacements: Array<string | number>` - Array with the replacements for `{n}` variables.

```js
i18n.npgettext(
  'info',
  '{0}, You have {1} message',
  '{0}, You have {1} messages',
  6,
  ['John', 6],
);
// => John, You have 6 messages
```

### `createI18nMiddleware`

An ExpressJS middleware that creates an `I18n` instance per request.

This middleware defines the following properties:

- `req.translations`: A translations object (already located) to use over the request life. It will use `req.locale` or `req.platform.countryId` to set the appropiate translations.
- `req.i18n`: An instance of I18n module with `req.translations` loaded and ready to use.

> [!TIP]
>
> To know the structure of the **translations** object, and which locales are valid, you can refer to their type definitions [here](./src/types.ts).

#### `createI18nMiddleware` Initialization

```js
import { createI18nMiddleware } from '@meli/i18n/middleware';

router.use(createI18nMiddleware({ srcPath: 'app' }));
```

##### `createI18nMiddleware` Initialization Options

| Property                    |   Type    |   Default   | Description                                                                                                     |
| --------------------------- | :-------: | :---------: | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `srcPath`                   | `string`  |   `./app`   | Path to the directory where the `translations` directory will be located.                                       |
| `notifyMissingTranslations` | `boolean` |   `false`   | Send metrics notifying about missing translations.                                                              |
| `I18nClass`                 |  `I18n`   | `undefined` | Class extending de `I18n` class to add new methods or functionalities. Constructor parameters can't be changed. |

#### `createI18nMiddleware` - Example using `req.platform.countryId`

```js
import { createI18nMiddleware } from '@meli/i18n/middleware';

router.use(
  createI18nMiddleware({
    srcPath: './path/to/i18n',
  }),
);

router.get('/something', (req, res) => {
  // Use translations...
  console.log(req.translations);
  console.log(req.i18n.gettext('Hello!'));
  // ¡Hola!
});
```

#### `createI18nMiddleware` - Example using `req.locale`

```js
import { createI18nMiddleware } from '@meli/i18n/middleware';

router.use((req, res, next) => {
  req.locale = 'pt-BR';
  next();
});

router.use(createI18nMiddleware());

router.get('/something', (req, res) => {
  // Use translations...
  console.log(req.translations);
  console.log(req.i18n.gettext('Hello!'));
  // Olá!
});
```

### Add Lib Translations

You can add translations to an app from a library by declaring a `postinstall` script which invokes a CLI tool to retrieve the lib translations.

To add translations to your library so that they can be added to the app which uses your lib, follow these steps:

1. Install and use the [I18n CLI](https://github.com/melisource/fury_node-i18n-dev-tools) to manage your translations.
2. Add a `postinstall` script in your `package.json` to add the lib's translations to the app when installing the library.

   ```json
   {
     "name": "my-awesome-lib",
     "scripts": {
       "postinstall": "meli-i18n add-translations ./path/to/translations"
     }
   }
   ```

3. Create a test version of your lib and install it in an app to validate:
   - The file `./node_modules/.meli/i18n/lib-translations.txt` has been created.
   - The path to your translations was added.

> [!NOTE]
>
> It's important for your translations directory to follow the same structure as the one created by the I18n CLI when downloading the translations.
>
> If the structure is not respected, then the translations may not be included.
